requirement: Protocol spec, Go codec library, unit tests

tasks.title: Native protocol spec + codec library + tests
tasks.description:
All-in-one: spec doc, Go codec, and tests for the native binary protocol.

Part A — Write docs/native-protocol-spec.md:
- Binary message format: 14-byte header (msg_type uint16, length uint32, sequence uint64)
- Message types: Login(0x01), Logout(0x02), Heartbeat(0x03), NewOrder(0x10), CancelOrder(0x11), ModifyOrder(0x12), ExecutionReport(0x20), OrderBookUpdate(0x21), Reject(0x30)
- Field encoding: fixed-length, big-endian, strings padded with spaces to fixed width
- Session: login with CompID+password, sequence numbers per session, heartbeat every 30s
- Keep spec under 500 lines

Part B — Create src/securities-service/internal/protocol/:
- message.go: Header struct (MsgType uint16, Length uint32, Sequence uint64), Message interface with Encode()/Type()/Sequence()
- codec.go: Encoder (Write Message to io.Writer), Decoder (Read Message from io.Reader), header encode/decode with encoding/binary BigEndian
- messages.go: LoginMsg, LogoutMsg, HeartbeatMsg, NewOrderMsg (InstrumentID[12], Side byte, OrderType byte, Quantity uint32, Price uint64 as fixed-point *10000), CancelOrderMsg, ExecutionReportMsg, RejectMsg — each implements Message interface
- All zero-dep (encoding/binary + io only)

Part C — Create src/securities-service/internal/protocol/codec_test.go:
- TestHeader_EncodeDecode — round-trip header
- TestLoginMsg_EncodeDecode — round-trip with CompID + password
- TestNewOrderMsg_EncodeDecode — round-trip with all fields, verify fixed-point price
- TestExecutionReport_EncodeDecode
- TestHeartbeat_EncodeDecode
- TestDecoder_MalformedInput — truncated header → error
- TestDecoder_UnknownMsgType → error
- TestSequenceNumber_Validation — out-of-order → detectable
- TestPriceFixedPoint — 850.25 → uint64 8502500, round-trip

Go at /usr/local/go/bin/go. Run: export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin && cd src/securities-service && go build ./... && go test ./internal/protocol/... -race -cover -v

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T1.md
